source: pred.gg public profile + own aggregate baselines

archetype.label: Closet Murdock Main
archetype.receipt: 53.9% over 138 games on Murdock — about 5.8 more wins per 100 games than the average Murdock player. The wide pool hides a weapon.

entries.change: Move the support games to offlane
Queue offlane instead of the usual mix
entries.receipt: 54.2% there vs 51.6% overall, 135 games of evidence
53.9% over 138 games on Murdock vs 51.6% overall

ledger.note: wins per 100 games spent on each change; entries overlap and do not sum

plan: Queue offlane as primary. It is Mr_Meat31's best role at 54.2% over 135 games (overall is 51.6%). Set support as secondary, not the other way around.
Comfort-hero rule for the climb: Murdock. Compared with the average player on the same hero, Mr_Meat31 wins about 5.8 more games per 100 on it.
Mr_Meat31's champion pool is 12+ heroes wide and the top three are only 41% of the games. Gold-to-Platinum climbs are almost always pool-narrowing stories: aim for 70%+ of games on the top three.
The math: Mr_Meat31 is at 589 VP; Platinum III starts at 900. The all-time peak is 867.
honesty: personal winrates are shrunk toward the player's own average (small heater samples do not count as mains)
field baselines are all-ranks aggregates from our current-patch match sample, not Gold-bracket-specific yet
winrate edges are observational; they say where the player wins, not why

insights.title: Xeebs brings out Mr_Meat31's best
insights.finding: Mr_Meat31's record alongside Xeebs is 54% over 246 ranked games — the strongest pairing in the stack. When only two of the squad queue, make it this pair.

insights.title: For the group chat
insights.finding: 1 pentakill in real 5v5s and a longest spree of 19 (plus 12 more in ARAM and brawls — the group chat can rule on whether those count). Mr_Meat31 has receipts.

insights.title: Mr_Meat31's lean, relative to the squad: objective damage
insights.finding: Mr_Meat31's profile sits mid-pack on most axes; the clearest lean is objective damage at 0.8x the squad average. Steady is a style too — the lineup can build around predictability.
